name: Buryat (Russia)
notes: Buryat (буряад хэлэн), a Mongolic language of the Republic of Buryatia, Russia, written since 1939 in the Russian Cyrillic alphabet plus Өө, Үү and Һһ /h/. Vowel harmony and a phonemic length contrast: long vowels are written by DOUBLING the vowel letter (аа ээ ии оо өө уу үү) and the sequences ⟨ай ой үй⟩ are realised as the long vowels [ɛː œː yː]; all of these are given as multigraph keys and win under maximal-munch matching. Unstressed /a/ and /ɔ/ reduce to [ɐ] and unstressed /ɤ/ to [ə] (recorded in `allophones`, not applied by the map). Only letters whose IPA value is stated by the cited sources are mapped: п, к, ф, щ, ы, ъ, ь and the iotated letters е ё ю я — all marginal or loanword-only in Buryat — are deliberately LEFT OUT because no source consulted gives them an explicit Buryat IPA value, and no value was invented for them. ⟨ь⟩/⟨ъ⟩ in any case mark palatalisation rather than a segment. The Mongolian script (Mongolia/China) and the 1931–1939 Latin orthography are out of scope. INPUT CONTRACT: Cyrillic Buryat text, Unicode NFC. The iotated letters е /jɛ~jœ/, ё /jo/, ю /ju/, я /ja/ and ы /iː~ei/ are mapped from the Buryat row of the Wikipedia 'Cyrillic alphabets' table and the Wiktionary entry буряад /bʊ.rʲaːd/. The letters в к п ф ц ч щ ъ are not used in native Buryat words and no source read here gives them a Buryat IPA value, so they are deliberately left unmapped.
